title: Citrusy Aerated Algerian Shrimp Paste Tagine
description: A weeknight Moroccan-inspired stew made with shrimp paste, lemons, and spices, served in a bento box, all egg-free and aerated for a light and delicious meal.
instructions: Heat the olive oil in the ttukbaegi earthenware cooking vessel over medium heat.
Add the chopped onion and cook until it starts to soften, about 3 minutes.
Add the minced garlic and cook for another minute, until fragrant.
Add the ground cumin, coriander, ginger, cayenne pepper, salt, and black pepper, and stir to combine.
Add the shrimp paste and cook for 1-2 minutes, until it starts to break down.
Add the lemon juice and water, and stir to combine.
Bring the mixture to a simmer.
Reduce the heat to low and cook, covered, for 10-15 minutes, or until the flavors have melded together and the shrimp paste has dissolved.
Stir in the cooked rice and cook for an additional 2-3 minutes, until heated through.
Serve the tagine hot, garnished with chopped fresh herbs if desired.
Transfer the tagine to a bento box and serve immediately.
Strain the liquid from the tagine and discard any solids before serving, if desired.
Note: The ttukbaegi earthenware cooking vessel is essential to this recipe, as it allows for the aerated, light texture of the tagine.
Note: You can also make this recipe in a Dutch oven or heavy pot, but it will not have the same texture as the ttukbaegi.
Note: Traditionally, this dish is cooked over an open flame, but you can also cook it on the stovetop.
Note: If you can't find shrimp paste, you can substitute it with a combination of fish sauce and chili flakes.
Note: If you prefer a spicier tagine, you can add more cayenne pepper or use hot sauce to taste.
Note: You can also add other ingredients to the tagine, such as chopped veggies or nuts, to suit your taste preferences.
